quiz_items.question: For Class IIIb/IV laser products, what are the remote interlock connector requirements? (Select all that apply)
options: Required on all Class IIIb and IV systems
Circuit must prevent access when terminals are open
Electrical potential limited to 130 volts rms
quiz_items.explanation: Remote interlock connectors are required on Class IIIb/IV systems. When terminals open, access prevented. Voltage limited to 130V rms. Shorted mating connector may be provided for operation without remote interlock.

quiz_items.question: Why are snap-action spring switches NOT acceptable as fail-safe interlocks for Class IIIb/IV products?
options: They are subject to short-circuit failure
quiz_items.explanation: Snap-action spring switches are subject to short-circuit failure and cannot serve as fail-safe safety interlocks. However, they could be used in series with a second switch or solenoid-operated latch.
